I agree with the final consensus presented which includes both option A (Retinal Ganglion Cells) and Option B (Bipolar Neurons). However, adding nuance, it’s important to recognize that while retinal ganglion cells indeed project directly via optic nerves to the thalamus, they do so after receiving processed inputs from bipolar cells within the inner nuclear layer. Therefore, although not traditionally classified as first-order neurons themselves, bipolar cells play an indispensable role in initiating more sophisticated visual computations needed beyond simple light detection—a key aspect sometimes overlooked in concise summaries focusing solely on projections.
